opinion.h: RISER v. WARDEN, CALIFORNIA STATE PRISON. APPEAL FROM THE SUPREME COURT OF CALIFORNIA. No. 331, Misc. Decided February 24, 1959.
opinion.p: Appeal dismissed for want of a substantial federal question.
Stanley Mosk, Attorney General of California, Clarence A. Linn, Chief Assistant Attorney General, and Arlo E. Smith, Deputy Attorney General, for appellee.
The motion to dismiss is granted and the appeal is dismissed for want of a substantial federal question.
358 U.S. 646 JOHNSON v. OHIO. APPEAL FROM THE COURT OF APPEALS OF OHIO, MONTGOMERY COUNTY. No. 428, Misc. Decided February 24, 1959.
The appeal is dismissed. Treating the papers whereon the appeal was taken as a petition for writ of certiorari, certiorari is denied.
